entry.title: Re: Minimize Custom condition types and account keys for...
entry.content:
Hi @kirankumardara ,

One alternative would be to leverage Process Variants instead of creating a complete set of custom condition types, account keys, and pricing procedure entries for each promotion.

With Process Variants, you can drive the G/L account determination based on the Process Variant used during settlement, which significantly reduces the need to duplicate RES1, REA1, REA5, RES2 and their associated account keys.

The main downside is that you would typically need to create separate Condition Contracts for each G/L account determination scenario, since the Process Variant is defined at the contract level. Depending on the number of promotions and how your business manages contracts, this may or may not be acceptable.

If the business requirement is to have 26 different promotional buckets posting to 26 different G/L accounts within the same Condition Contract, then the standard approach usually involves separate condition types and account determination logic. However, if the promotions can be grouped into separate contracts, Process Variants can be an elegant way to simplify the configuration and reduce the complexity of the pricing procedures.

Best regards,
Gauthier VICTOR

entry.title: AI in Procurement - A visual walkthrough
entry.content:
Most procurement teams are sitting on a goldmine of data they cannot access, contracts they cannot track, and risks they cannot see until it is too late.
 
This video follows one CPO — thirteen minutes before a board meeting, three unanswered questions from her CFO — and shows exactly how AI changes that equation. Not through theory, but through the four capabilities that are transforming procurement right now: spend visibility, smarter sourcing, contract intelligence, and supplier risk management.
 
The numbers speak for themselves. Ninety-seven percent spend classification accuracy. Ninety percent faster negotiation preparation. Three point two million euros saved by seeing a supply chain risk before it became a crisis.
 
If your procurement function is still running on spreadsheets and quarterly reports, this is worth six minutes of your time.

 

Presentation

entry.title: Re: Zertifizierung zur FI-Beraterin für die Cloud Edition
entry.content:
Hallo Laura,

zuerst einmal: ja - eine SAP Learning Hub Lizenz ist der beste Weg zur Zertifizierung und diese auch innerhalb eines Jahres mit einem sogen. Stay Certified Assessment um 1 Jahr zu verlängern. Die Kosten liegen bei 1260,--€ bzw.  1488,-- USD.

Für Financial Accounting bieten wir für SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition die Zertifizierung C_TS4FI an: https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-sap-s-4hana-for-financial-accounting
Für SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition die Zertifizierung C_S4CFI: https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-implementation-consultant-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-financial-accounting-1 
Beide Zertifizierungen sind sogen. System-based Zertifizierungen, d. h. das Wissen wird direkt am SAP-System abgeprüft.

Viele Grüße, Dieter

entry.title: Re: Storage Location not mandatory in PO
entry.content:
Dear @9657979991,

In Public Cloud making Storage Location mandatory in Purchase Orders should be enforced using the released BAdI Final Check before Saving Purchase Order (BD_MMPUR_FINAL_CHECK_PO)

The document type field selection key, such as NBF, may not be sufficient because purchasing field control is influenced by multiple field selection factors. Therefore, the recommended approach is to implement a save-time validation. If Storage Location is blank for a relevant PO item the BAdI raises an error and prevents the purchase order from being saved.

Ready to use implementation:

* Structure for messages

DATA: ls_message LIKE LINE OF messages.

* Check all PO items

LOOP AT purchaseorderitem INTO DATA(ls_po_item).

* Apply the check only where Storage Location is relevant

  IF ls_po_item-material IS NOT INITIAL

     AND ls_po_item-plant IS NOT INITIAL

     AND ls_po_item-storagelocation IS INITIAL.

    CLEAR ls_message.

    ls_message-messagetype = 'E'.

    ls_message-messagevariable1 =

      |Storage Location is mandatory for PO item { ls_po_item-purchaseorderitem }.|.

APPEND ls_message TO messages.

ENDIF.

ENDLOOP.

Attempting to add a Purchase Order document with an empty Storage Location results in an Error

Summary:

Standard configuration via NBF alone is not reliable for this requirement. The clean Public Cloud approach is to enforce the rule with BD_MMPUR_FINAL_CHECK_PO

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Intercompany STO Return Process for Damaged Goods with Automatic Credit/Debit Note Creation
entry.content:
Business Scenario

  • Company Code 1004 creates a Stock Transport Order (STO) to Company Code 1003.
  • An intercompany sales order is automatically created in Company Code 1003 with Company Code 1004 as the customer.
  • Company Code 1003 delivers the goods and posts the goods issue.
  • Company Code 1004 receives the goods and posts the goods receipt.
  • Intercompany billing is completed in Company Code 1003.
  • Supplier invoice is automatically created/booked in Company Code 1004.

Issue

After goods receipt, Company Code 1004 identified that the received material is damaged and needs to be returned to Company Code 1003.

Requirement

Need to understand and configure the standard return process in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud for an Intercompany STO scenario with the following expected outcome:

  1. Company Code 1004 creates a return request/return order against the original STO.
  2. Damaged material is physically returned from Company Code 1004 to Company Code 1003.
  3. Return delivery and goods movement are posted.
  4. Intercompany billing documents are reversed/credited.
  5. A credit note is generated for Company Code 1004 (customer side).
  6. A corresponding debit note/credit memo adjustment is automatically generated in Company Code 1003 (supplying company).
  7. Financial postings are cleared against the original intercompany invoice and supplier invoice.

Questions

  • What is the standard SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud process for Intercompany STO returns after billing has already been completed?
  • Which scope items/apps should be used?
  • Is a Return Purchase Order, Customer Return, or Intercompany Returns process required?
  • Can credit memo and debit memo documents be created automatically from the return process?
  • What are the required configuration and master data settings?

Example

Supplying Company: 1003

Receiving Company: 1004

Original Flow:
STO → Intercompany Sales Order → Outbound Delivery → PGI → Goods Receipt → Intercompany Billing → Supplier Invoice

Required Return Flow:
Return Request → Return Delivery → Return Goods Receipt by 1003 → Credit Memo to 1004 → Corresponding Debit/Credit Adjustment in 1003

entry.title: Extending Contract Account Notes to SAP S/4HANA Customer Engagement
entry.content:
Introduction

In SAP S4HANA Utilities implementations, Contract Account (CA) notes are a valuable source of contextual information for customer service representatives. Contract Account notes in SAP Utilities (IS-U) are stored at the database level but are not natively exposed through the BOL framework as a relation to the UtilsContrAcctPartner object. To enable this, a new custom Business Layer object — UtilsContrAcctPartnerNotes — has been created for component ISUMDE. This blog post walks through the end-to-end technical approach used to surface CA notes as a display-only section on the WebUI, from BOL modeling all the way through UI component wiring.

Step 1 — Defining the Data Model and CDS Views

The foundation of the solution begins with the creation of the data structures and CDS views that support the new BOL entity. These artefacts provide the metadata, business-layer representation, and query capabilities required to expose Contract Account notes within the CRM WebUI.

The following ABAP structures are created as part of the implementation:

  • ZCRMS4S_IU_CA_NOTES_KEY – Defines the key structure used to uniquely identify a Contract Account note record.

  • ZCRMS4S_IU_CA_NOTES_BL_DATA – Contains the non-key business data attributes associated with the note.

  • ZCRMS4S_IU_CA_NOTES_ATTR – Serves as the main attribute structure by combining the key and business data structures. It also includes additional fields such as Contract Account, Business Partner, and note text content to support BOL relationships and UI requirements.

  • ZCRMS4S_IU_CA_NOTES_BL – Represents the business layer structure used during BOL object configuration. For this instance, the structure is taken the same as the Attribute Structure
  • ZCRMS4S_IU_CA_NOTES_QRY – Defines the query structure that powers search and retrieval operations for the custom BOL entity. For this instance, the structure is taken the same as the Attribute Structure.

To enable efficient search and data retrieval, two CDS views are introduced:

  • ZCRMS4_IU_I_CANOTES – An interface view that reads Contract Account note data directly from the underlying SAP IS-U database table - STXH.
  • ZCRMS4_IU_C_CANOTE – A consumption view built on top of the interface view, designed specifically for use by the BOL query framework.

Together, these structures and CDS views form the data foundation for the custom BOL/GenIL enhancement, enabling Contract Account notes to be seamlessly surfaced within the CE WebUI.

Step 2 — Implementing the Interaction, Query and Business Layer Classes

With the data model in place, the next step is to implement the runtime logic required to expose and retrieve Contract Account notes through the BOL/GenIL framework. This is achieved by creating custom ABAP classes across the Interaction Layer and Business Layer, each extending the appropriate SAP standard base class.

The following classes are introduced:

  • ZCL_IU_IL_CA_NOTES (extends CL_CRMS4_IU_IL_ABSTR_ROOT) – The Interaction Layer root entity class responsible for handling attribute access, property behavior, and entity-level interactions.
  • ZCL_IU_IL_CA_NOTES_QUERY (extends CL_CRMS4_IU_IL_ABSTR_QUERY) – The Interaction Layer query class that manages search requests and query execution for the custom business object.
  • ZCL_IU_BL_CA_NOTES (extends CL_CRMS4_IU_BL_BASE) – The Business Layer implementation class that provides data access logic and serves as the bridge between the BOL framework and the underlying CDS-based data model.

A key enhancement within the Business Layer is the implementation of the IF_CRMS4_IU_BL_ACCESS~QUERY method in ZCL_IU_BL_CA_NOTES. This method is extended to execute search operations against the custom CDS views, enabling the retrieval of Contract Account notes based on the criteria supplied by the CRM WebUI.

  METHOD if_crms4_iu_bl_access~query.
    TYPES: text_line(132) TYPE c.
    DATA: lt_lines                TYPE comt_text_lines_t,
          ls_lines                LIKE LINE OF lt_lines,
          lv_vkont                TYPE vkont_kk,
          lv_gpart                TYPE bu_partner,
          lt_text_table           TYPE TABLE OF text_line,
          lv_string               TYPE string,
          lv_str                  TYPE string,
          lv_blanks               TYPE i,
          lt_selection_parameters TYPE genilt_selection_parameter_tab,
          lt_result               TYPE TABLE OF zcrms4s_iu_ca_notes_qry,
          lv_tdname               TYPE tdobname.

    FIELD-SYMBOLS: <str>     TYPE text_line,
                   <ls_data> TYPE zcrms4s_iu_ca_notes_qry,
                   <datas>   TYPE ANY TABLE.
*** removing the parameters gpart & vkont and adding them to Tdname ****
    lt_selection_parameters = it_selection_parameters.
    LOOP AT lt_selection_parameters ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_param>).
      IF <ls_param>-attr_name EQ 'GPART'.
        lv_gpart = |{ <ls_param>-low ALPHA = IN }|.
        CLEAR: <ls_param>.
      ENDIF.
      IF <ls_param>-attr_name EQ 'VKONT'.
        lv_vkont = |{ <ls_param>-low ALPHA = IN }|.
        CLEAR: <ls_param>.
      ENDIF.
      IF lv_gpart IS NOT INITIAL AND lv_vkont IS NOT INITIAL .
        <ls_param>-attr_name = 'TDNAME'.
        <ls_param>-sign = 'I'.
        <ls_param>-option = 'EQ'.
        <ls_param>-low = lv_vkont && lv_gpart.
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.
    DELETE lt_selection_parameters WHERE attr_name IS INITIAL.
    TRY.
        CALL METHOD super->if_crms4_iu_bl_access~query
          EXPORTING
            iv_max_hits             = iv_max_hits
            it_selection_parameters = lt_selection_parameters
            it_convertion           = it_convertion
            ir_message_container    = ir_message_container
            iv_object_name          = iv_object_name
            iv_no_fillup_attributes = abap_false
          IMPORTING
            et_table                = lt_result
            ev_selection_changed    = ev_selection_changed
            ev_only_keys_selected   = ev_only_keys_selected.
      CATCH cx_crms4_iu_base.
    ENDTRY.
    LOOP AT lt_result ASSIGNING <ls_data>.
      CALL FUNCTION 'READ_TEXT'
        EXPORTING
          id       = <ls_data>-tdid
          language = <ls_data>-tdspras
          name     = <ls_data>-tdname
          object   = <ls_data>-tdobject
        TABLES
          lines    = lt_lines
        EXCEPTIONS
          OTHERS   = 0.

      CALL FUNCTION 'LANGUAGE_CODE_SAP_TO_ISO'
        EXPORTING
          sap_code = <ls_data>-tdspras
        IMPORTING
          iso_code = <ls_data>-languiso
        EXCEPTIONS
          OTHERS   = 0.
      READ TABLE lt_lines INDEX 1 INTO ls_lines.
      <ls_data>-tdformat = ls_lines-tdformat.
      CALL FUNCTION 'CONVERT_ITF_TO_STREAM_TEXT'
        EXPORTING
          language    = sy-langu
        TABLES
          itf_text    = lt_lines
          text_stream = lt_text_table.

      IF sy-tabix GT 1.
        CLEAR lv_blanks.
        LOOP AT lt_text_table ASSIGNING <str>.
          MOVE <str> TO lv_str.
          SHIFT lv_str BY lv_blanks PLACES RIGHT.
          CONCATENATE lv_string lv_str INTO lv_string.
          lv_blanks = 132 - strlen( <str> ).
        ENDLOOP.
      ELSE.
        LOOP AT lt_text_table ASSIGNING <str>.
          lv_string = <str>.
        ENDLOOP.
      ENDIF.
      <ls_data>-tdform  = 'SYSTEM'.
      <ls_data>-tdstyle = 'SYSTEM'.
      <ls_data>-tdline = lv_string.
      MOVE cl_gstext_tools=>transform_itf_to_itfstring( lt_lines ) TO <ls_data>-conc_formatted_lines.
      IF lv_gpart IS NOT INITIAL AND lv_vkont IS NOT INITIAL.
        <ls_data>-gpart = lv_gpart.
        <ls_data>-vkont = lv_vkont.
      ELSE.
        <ls_data>-vkont = <ls_data>-tdname(12). 
        <ls_data>-gpart = substring( val = <ls_data>-tdname off = 12 len = 10 ).
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.
    et_table = CORRESPONDING #( lt_result ).
*** update the data retrieved in the global container
*    GR_DATAS
    ASSIGN gr_datas->* TO <datas>.
    <datas> = CORRESPONDING #( et_table ).
  ENDMETHOD.

 

Together, these classes provide the runtime framework necessary for querying, retrieving, and exposing Contract Account note information through the custom BOL entity.

Step 3 — Registering the BOL Object in the Framework

After creating the required data structures and implementing the Interaction Layer and Business Layer classes, the new BOL entity must be registered within the CRM framework. This is accomplished through transaction SM34 using the view cluster CRMC_IUIL_OBJPROP for the ISUMDE component.

The registration process consists of four key configuration areas:

1. Root Object Registration

The custom entity is registered as a related object under the existing UtilsContrAcctPartner root object, enabling Contract Account notes to be accessed within the established business object hierarchy. During this step:

  • ZCL_IU_IL_CA_NOTES is assigned as the Interaction Layer implementation class.
  • The custom key and attribute structures are linked to the object definition.
  • The object is exposed as UtilsContrAcctPartnerNotes within the BOL model.

2. Query Object Registration

To enable search and retrieval capabilities, a dedicated query object is configured using ZCL_IU_IL_CA_NOTES_QUERY as the Query Interaction Layer class.

The configuration specifies:

  • UtilsContrAcctPartner as the root object.
  • UtilsContrAcctPartnerNotes as the result object returned by the query execution.

3. Dynamic Query Object Configuration

A corresponding Dynamic Query object is created using the same configuration principles. This allows the framework to support dynamic search scenarios and runtime query execution for the new entity.

4. BOL Relation Definitions

To enable seamless navigation between business objects, two BOL relations are defined:

  • A forward relation from UtilsContrAcctPartner to UtilsContrAcctPartnerNotes, allowing Contract Account notes to be retrieved from the parent object.
  • A reverse relation from UtilsContrAcctPartnerNotes back to UtilsContrAcctPartner, supporting bidirectional navigation within the BOL model.

Once the configuration is saved and activated, the newly registered entity, query objects, and relationships can be validated using transaction GENIL_MODEL_BROWSER, ensuring that the custom BOL object is correctly integrated into the CRM framework.

Step 4 — Configuring the Business Layer

With the BOL entity successfully registered, the next step is to configure the corresponding Business Layer (BL) components. The Business Layer serves as the data access framework behind the BOL object and must be aligned with the entity structure, CDS views, and relationships defined in the previous steps.

The configuration is maintained within the Business Layer customization transactions for the ISUMDE component and includes the following activities:

1. Business Layer Object Definition

The custom Business Layer object is created as a Root Object Type, establishing the primary entry point for data retrieval. During this configuration:

  • The custom CDS view is assigned as the data source.
  • The Business Layer structure is linked to the object definition.
  • The custom Business Layer implementation class (ZCL_IU_BL_CA_NOTES) is assigned to handle runtime processing and data access.

2. Query Registration

Custom queries are registered within the Define Queries configuration section. Each query is mapped to its corresponding CDS view, enabling the Business Layer to execute searches and return Contract Account note records based on the criteria supplied by the CRM WebUI.

3. Relation Configuration

The relationships established in the BOL model are replicated within the Business Layer through the Define Relations configuration. Both forward and backward relations are defined here along with the relation type and associated query objects.

4. Relation Key and Parameter Mapping

To support accurate data retrieval and object navigation, relation keys and parameter mappings are configured for each relationship. These mappings define how key fields are transferred between related objects, ensuring that Contract Account notes are correctly associated with their parent Contract Account and Business Partner entities.

Once completed, the Business Layer configuration provides the underlying data access and navigation framework required for the custom BOL entity to function seamlessly within the CRM WebUI.

Step 5 — Text Determination for Contract Account Note Types

To leverage the standard GSTEST component, a dedicated Text Determination Procedure is configured for Contract Account note types associated with text object FKKVKP. The note types included in this procedure define the set of notes that are retrieved and displayed within the WebUI. This configuration ensures that only relevant and business-approved note categories are presented to end users, providing a controlled and consistent user experience.

Step 6 — WebUI Configuration: Form and Window Setup

To enable the display of Contract Account (CA) notes within the Contract Account view, several WebUI configurations activities and component enhancements are implemented to make the view available with the Contract Account screen.

Form Configuration

  • A Simple Create/Change form is configured in the Define Forms section, with form titles maintained.

  • A dedicated Form Mask is created in the BSP Workbench, specifying the Window Name, Component Name, Inbound Plug, and Context Node.

  • The form is registered under the Form Overview (a custom form is created by copying standard form UCAP_OV), where the corresponding Object Determination Profile and relation are assigned.

 

  • The Object Determination Profile is subsequently incorporated into the Object Hierarchy configuration

     

    Component Development (IUFMUCAP)

    • A new window, ZNotes, is developed within component IUFMUCAP to host the note display functionality. 
    •  
    • A component usage is configured in the Runtime Repository to reuse the standard SAP text component GSTEXT.

       

    • The configured component usages are assigned to the ZNotes window through the Runtime Repository, enabling seamless integration of the note display functionality within the Contract Account view.

       

Step 7 — Component Controller Enhancements and Context Node Binding

The Component Controller of IUFMUCAP is enhanced to facilitate data transfer between the Business Object Layer (BOL) and the reused GSTEXT component. To support this integration, two additional context nodes are introduced:

  • TEXTATTR (Value Node):
    This node stores text attribute information. The IF_BSP_MODEL~INIT method is redefined to assign the appropriate structure, leveraging the existing definition provided by the reused GSTEXT component. The ON_NEW_FOCUS method is defined to provide data to the added collection

METHOD on_new_focus.

    DATA: current       TYPE REF TO if_bol_bo_property_access,
          entity        TYPE REF TO cl_crm_bol_entity,
          ls_attributes TYPE crmst_uiu_text_attr.

    current = collection_wrapper->get_current( ).
    CHECK current IS BOUND.

** TEXTPROC
    SELECT SINGLE textprocedure FROM comc_text_p INTO (ls_attributes-textproc)
      WHERE object = 'FKKVKP'.

* TEXTOBJ
    ls_attributes-textobj = 'FKKVKP'.                       "#EC NOTEXT

* LANGU_ATTR_NAME
    ls_attributes-langu_attr_name = 'LANGUISO'.              "#EC NOTEXT

* IDENT_ATTR_NAME
    ls_attributes-ident_attr_name = 'TDID'.              "#EC NOTEXT

* LINES_ATTR_NAME
    ls_attributes-lines_attr_name = 'TDLINE'.               "#EC NOTEXT

* PARENT_RELATION
    ls_attributes-parent_relation = 'UtilsContrAcctPartnerNotesRel'. "#EC NOTEXT

* formatting related attributes
    ls_attributes-lines_formatted_attr_name = 'CONC_FORMATTED_LINES'. "#EC NOTEXT
*  TDFORM and TDSTYLE are supported for accounts to provide formatting
    ls_attributes-form_attr_name  = 'TDFORM'.               "#EC NOTEXT
    ls_attributes-style_attr_name = 'TDSTYLE'.              "#EC NOTEXT
    ls_attributes-use_formatting  = 'X'.                    "#EC NOTEXT

* PARENT_ENTITY
    TRY.
        entity ?= focus_bo.
        ls_attributes-parent_entity = entity.
      CATCH cx_sy_move_cast_error .
    ENDTRY.

    current->set_properties( ls_attributes ).

    me->collection_wrapper->publish_current( ).
  • TEXT (Model Node):
    This node is bound to the custom BOL object UtilsContrAcctPartnerNotes. The ON_NEW_FOCUS method is implemented to populate the collection by utilizing the relation configured within the Business Layer.
METHOD on_new_focus.
    DATA: lv_collection TYPE REF TO if_bol_bo_col,
          entity        TYPE REF TO cl_crm_bol_entity.

*   get collection of dependent nodes
    TRY.
        entity ?= focus_bo.
      CATCH cx_sy_move_cast_error. " Create
    ENDTRY.
    TRY.
        lv_collection = entity->get_related_entities(
          iv_relation_name = 'UtilsContrAcctPartnerNotesRel' ).
      CATCH cx_crm_genil_model_error.
*       should never happen
        EXIT.
      CATCH cx_sy_ref_is_initial.
    ENDTRY.
*  CHECK lv_collection IS BOUND.
    me->collection_wrapper->set_collection( lv_collection ).
  ENDMETHOD.​

Additionally, the WD_USAGE_INITIALIZE method is redefined to establish the context binding between IUFMUCAP and GSTEXT. This enables the collection data maintained in the parent component to be passed seamlessly to the reused text component, ensuring the relevant Contract Account notes are retrieved and displayed correctly within the WebUI.

By implementing the above configuration and development steps, the notes maintained in CAA3 can be seamlessly displayed within the WebUI as a dedicated tab (configured as Remarks) on the Contract Account screen.

Conclusion

This approach demonstrates how custom Business Layer objects can be seamlessly integrated into the SAP S/4HANA Utilities Customer Engagement WebUI by leveraging the standard BOL framework, component reuse, and WebUI configuration capabilities. By following established SAP design patterns, we can extend standard functionality in a maintainable and upgrade-friendly manner while minimizing modifications to the core application.

The same implementation pattern can be applied to a wide range of custom business objects and use cases, enabling efficient integration of additional business data and processes into the Customer Engagement WebUI.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud – Alternative to Document Type ER for Intercompany Travel Expense Recharge
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and using the following scope items for cross-company cost allocation and intercompany billing processes:

  • 4AN – Intercompany Billing for Cross-Company Cost Accounting Postings
  • 4AU – Project Control Intercompany
  • 16T – Intercompany Processes – Project-Based Services

During our analysis of the standard process for cross-company cost recharging (especially travel expense recharges), we noticed that the SAP Best Practice documentation for scope item 4AU uses document type ER – Manual Travel Expenses when posting intercompany expenses.

In the process step "Post Cross-Company Expenses", a supplier invoice is created using document type ER and posted to a WBS element before the subsequent intercompany billing process is executed.

Our Challenge

In our system landscape, travel expenses and other employee-related costs originate from upstream systems and are transferred to SAP via interfaces (e.g. Travel & Expense solutions).

As a result, users do not manually create supplier invoices in SAP using document type ER, as described in the Best Practice process.

When we use document type ER, we encounter problems with currency conversion as well as with advance payment invoices

Instead, the costs already exist in SAP as FI postings generated through interfaces.

Questions

We would like to understand what SAP-recommended alternatives exist for enabling intercompany travel expense recharges and other intercompany cost reallocations when document type ER cannot be used.

Specifically:

  1. Is the use of document type ER a technical prerequisite for generating the subsequent intercompany billing request in scope items 4AN / 4AU?
  2. We would very much like to use the SAP standard and implement a fully automated process
  3. Are there SAP Best Practices for intercompany travel expense recharges when the original costs are transferred from external expense management systems?
  4. Does anyone have experience implementing scope items 4AN, 4AU, and 16T in scenarios where travel expenses originate outside SAP and are integrated via interfaces?

Target Scenario

Our objective is to use the standard SAP intercompany billing functionality without requiring manual reposting of travel expenses through supplier invoices with document type ER.

We would appreciate any guidance, SAP Notes, Best Practices, or project experiences regarding this topic.

Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: Re: fixed quantity indicator in standard costing estimate
entry.content:
Hi Lakshmipathi,

Thank you for your detailed information, we followed the steps, we set the raw material in the finished product BOM, we set the fixed quantity indicator in the BOM and also set the costing lot size = 1. However, CK11N is still multiplying the quantity of the raw material based on the quantity of the finished product. When I create a production order the fixed indicator works, the raw material quantity remains the same regardless of the finish good quantity, it also works in transaction CS11 the raw material quantity remains the same regardless of the finish good quantity. Why is this not working in CK11N? Can you please help us? Are we missing any steps or is there another way?

entry.title: Journal entries with 0 interest amount are not displayed in "Manage Interest Calculation Runs" app a
entry.content:
[Component / Application]

  • Job: Schedule Interest Calculation Jobs

  • App: Manage Interest Calculation Runs

[Symptom / Actual Behavior] When executing the interest calculation via the "Schedule Interest Calculation Jobs" app, the source journal entry numbers are normally output and can be verified in the "Manage Interest Calculation Runs" app. However, for journal entries where the interest calculation process was executed but the resulting calculated interest amount was "0", they are not output or displayed in the "Manage Interest Calculation Runs" app.

[Questions]

  1. Is it the standard behavior (intended specification) of SAP S/4HANA Cloud that journal entries with a calculated interest amount of 0 are not displayed in the "Manage Interest Calculation Runs" app?

  2. If it is standard behavior, is there any configuration change or parameter setting available to output/display these journal entries with 0 interest amount in the app?

entry.title: Re: Storage Location not mandatory in PO
entry.content:
SAP provides  tools to customize purchase order (PO) validation and field control, ensuring compliance with business requirements.

  • The BAdI Check of Purchase Order Before Saving (BD_MMPUR_FINAL_CHECK_PO) allows users to implement custom validation logic before a PO is saved, enabling error detection and preventing data inconsistencies.
  • Additionally, the Change of Field Control for Purchase Order Header (MM_PUR_S4_PO_FLDCNTRL_SIMPLE_H) and Item (MM_PUR_S4_PO_FLDCNTRL_SIMPLE) functionalities enable administrators to modify field properties at both the header and item levels. Fields can be configured as hidden, read-only, mandatory, or optional, improving data accuracy and user experience.
  • These enhancements provide greater flexibility in PO management, supporting tailored configurations while maintaining system integrity. By leveraging these features, organizations can optimize procurement processes and ensure adherence to internal policies.

entry.title: Re: Technical setup for Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Regarding the question 3):
Please check the answer below:
The SAP Expert Portal Setup Managed Components contains the setup information for SAP Cloud ALM and the supported systems (and services). This includes how to configure the connection between SAP Cloud ALM and the managed system (service), displays any prerequisites, and defines how to configure data collection for the various monitoring use cases. Ensure the prerequisites are met, including implementing all relevant SAP Notes and follow the documented steps. Note: There are some videos captured in this area that may be helpful, but as new versions reach release, they can be 'less' current than the text information contained on the portal. Note: If problems are encountered, check Troubleshooting for ABAP Systems  to try to resolve the problem. If problems remain in the area of connection, log a support case for SV-CLM-INF-CON.

entry.title: Experiencing change in layout after upgrade
entry.content:
After the upgrade, the standard WDA tiles are no longer opening in a new tab. Prior to the upgrade, clicking on a tile would open it in a new tab (except for the Home page). However, post-upgrade, the tile now opens in the same tab, replacing the Home screen.

We tried all steps as suggested in SAP support but nothing worked.Below are the steps we followed.

Open transaction /n/UI2/FLP_CUS_CONF for client‑specific settings or /n/UI2/FLP_SYS_CONF for system‑wide settings.

Locate the parameters NAVIGATION_GUI_INPLACE and NAVIGATION_WDA_INPLACE.

Set NAVIGATION_WDA_INPLACE = false (and optionally NAVIGATION_GUI_INPLACE = false) to force WDA tiles to open in a new browser tab (ex‑place). Setting the value to true will keep the navigation in‑place (same tab).

Save the changes, clear the browser cache and restart the Fiori Launchpad session 

Even after all these settings we have seen same behaviour.

Kindly check and confirm if any settings are missing on our end to restore the system behavior to how it functioned prior to the upgrade.

entry.title: Integrate and Automate: Calling SAP Integrated Product Development Collaboration APIs from ABAP Code
entry.content:
SAP Integrated Product Development (SAP IPD) is a cloud-based Product Lifecycle Management solution running on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). It provides a rich set of Collaboration APIs that allow external systems, e.g. SAP S/4HANA landscapes, to interact programmatically with collaboration objects, like Documents, Reference Objects, or Workflows. Setting up the initial connection requires five easy steps:

 

1. Create Service Instance and Key

You do have an active SAP IPD subscription on SAP BTP.  Add a new service instance for SAP IPD Collaboration or choose an existing one. Create a Service Key for the new Connection, e.g. use the calling system's name as name for the key. Note properties endpoints:collab-service-url:url and uaa:clientid, uaa:clientsecret, and uaa:url.

 

2. Create OAuth2 Client Profile

For Authentication and Authorization a client profile is required. This has to be created only once and can be reused for all connections. Create an OAuth2 client profile like this:

    1. Start transaction SE80 and select your package.
    2. Right-click on the package name and choose Create / Others / OAuth2 Client Profile.
    3. Enter a client profile name.
    4. Enter HANA_CLOUD_PLATFORM as the type.
    5. Leave scope empty.
    6. Check Administration / No authorization check if required.
    7. Save and activate.

 

3. Configure OAuth2 Settings

Configure OAuth2 settings as describe din the following section. We are using client credentials identifying the API as TECHNICAL-USER in SAP IPD Collaboration:

    1. Run transaction SOAUTH2_CLIENT.
    2. Choose Create.
    3. Enter the client profile name you created previously.
    4. Enter the client ID uaa:clientid from service key.
    5. Choose OK.
    6. In General Settings, enter Token endpoint uaa:url, append /oauth/token.
    7. In Client Authentication, enter the client secret uaa:clientsecret from service key.
    8. Save.

 

4. Create SM59 Destination

We don't want to hardcode the connection destails in our code but create an HTTP destination instead:

    1. Start transaction SM59
    2. Choose Create.
    3. Enter a name for the destination.
    4. Select G HTTP connection to external server as the connection type.
    5. On the Technical Settings tab, enter the URL without schema from service key endpoints:collab-service-url:url in the Host field, set Port 443.
    6. On the Logon & Security tab choose OAuth Settings and select the OAuth profile you created.
    7. Enable SSL with ANONYM SSL Client (Anonymous).
    8. Save.

 

5. Call API programmatically

For our example we are using the POST Collaboration endpoint to create a new Collaboration. Use this sample code as reference:

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& ABAP Example: Create Collaboration via EPD_COLLABORATION OData V4 API
*& API: POST /odata/v4/CollaborationService_v1/Collaboration
*& Auth: OAuth 2.0 handled automatically via BTP Destination
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

PROGRAM ZMBIPD.

DATA: lo_http_client   TYPE REF TO if_http_client,
      lv_json_body     TYPE string,
      lv_response_body TYPE string,
      lv_status_code   TYPE i,
      lv_status_text   TYPE string.

*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* STEP 1: Create HTTP Client via Named Destination 'IPD_COLLABORATION'
* The destination must be configured in SM59 / BTP Cockpit,
* no hardcoded credentials or URLs required in ABAP source code.
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
cl_http_client=>create_by_destination(
EXPORTING
destination = 'IPD_COLLABORATION'
IMPORTING
client = lo_http_client
EXCEPTIONS
argument_not_found = 1
destination_not_found = 2
destination_no_authority = 3
plugin_not_active = 4
internal_error = 5
OTHERS = 6 ).

IF sy-subrc <> 0 OR lo_http_client IS NOT BOUND.
  WRITE: / 'Error creating HTTP client from destination IPD_COLLABORATION.'.
  WRITE: / 'SY-SUBRC:', sy-subrc.
  RETURN.
ENDIF.

*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* STEP 2: Set Request Path for OData V4 Collaboration Entity
* The base URL is defined in the destination config.
* Only the relative resource path is appended here.
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
lo_http_client->request->set_method( if_http_request=>co_request_method_post ).

lo_http_client->request->set_header_field(
name = '~request_uri'
value = '/odata/v4/CollaborationService_v1/Collaboration' ).

lo_http_client->request->set_header_field(
name = 'Content-Type'
value = 'application/json' ).

lo_http_client->request->set_header_field(
name = 'Accept'
value = 'application/json' ).

*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* STEP 3: Build JSON Request Body
* Adjust field names to match the Collaboration entity
* schema as published on SAP Business Accelerator Hub.
* Replace placeholder values with actual runtime data.
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
lv_json_body = '{' &&
'"name": "My Collaboration",' &&
'"description": "Created via ABAP OData V4 call",' &&
'"collaborationType": "DFLT"' &&
'}'.

lo_http_client->request->set_cdata( lv_json_body ).

*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* STEP 4: Send Request and Receive Response
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
lo_http_client->send(
EXCEPTIONS
http_communication_failure = 1
http_invalid_state = 2
OTHERS = 3 ).

IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  WRITE: / 'Error sending HTTP request.'.
  WRITE: / 'SY-SUBRC:', sy-subrc.
  lo_http_client->close( ).
  RETURN.
ENDIF.

lo_http_client->receive(
EXCEPTIONS
http_communication_failure = 1
http_invalid_state = 2
OTHERS = 3 ).

IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  WRITE: / 'Error receiving HTTP response.'.
  WRITE: / 'SY-SUBRC:', sy-subrc.
  lo_http_client->close( ).
  RETURN.
ENDIF.

*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* STEP 5: Handle Response
* HTTP 201 = Collaboration created successfully
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
lo_http_client->response->get_status(
IMPORTING
code = lv_status_code
reason = lv_status_text ).

lv_response_body = lo_http_client->response->get_cdata( ).

WRITE: / 'HTTP Status Code:', lv_status_code.
WRITE: / 'Status Text:', lv_status_text.

IF lv_status_code = 201.
  WRITE: / 'Collaboration created successfully.'.
  WRITE: / 'Response:', lv_response_body.
ELSE.
  WRITE: / 'Error creating Collaboration:'.
  WRITE: / lv_response_body.
ENDIF.

*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* STEP 6: Close HTTP Connection
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
lo_http_client->close(
EXCEPTIONS
http_invalid_state = 1
OTHERS = 2 ).

Executing above code will create a new Collaboration of type DFLT. In case of success it will return status HTTP 201 Created with message Collaboration created successfully. 

View created Collaboration in My Collaborations App

entry.title: Re: Incidencia en sistema TEST a la hora de contabilizar
entry.content:
Hi Alberto,

Short answer: Error means the system cannot resolve the complete set of currency types for company code 2830 in at least one ledger it is assigned to. Checking the company code currency (CBC) and the ledger-level currency types is not enough — the missing piece is almost always the company-code-specific currency-type-to-ledger assignment, or a config change that hasn't fully reached the TEST tenant.

What the error actually checks

When you post, the system reads the full list of active currency types for the company code + ledger combination (company code currency type 10, group currency type 30, and any freely defined / additional currency). If any one of those types is active for the ledger but not completely maintained for company code 2830, the set is "incomplete" and posting is blocked. Defining the type at the ledger level alone does not complete it — each type must also be assigned and (where applicable) given conversion settings per company code. See the currency model in SAP Help – Currencies.

Most likely cause, given what you've already verified (deduction — to confirm against your system)

The gap is in SSCUI 103379 – Define Currency Settings for Ledgers and Company Codes, specifically the company-code-level assignment step (currency types & ledger assignment per company code), not the global/ledger step you already checked. Two common patterns:

  • An additional / freely defined currency is active on the ledger, but for CC 2830 its conversion settings (source currency, exchange rate type, translation date) are missing — typical when 2830 was added or copied after the currency was introduced. Reference: KBA 2856805 and KBA 2977199.
  • CC 2830 is assigned to more than one ledger (leading 0L plus a non-leading ledger), and the currency assignment is complete for one ledger but incomplete for another.

See the example below of some standard currency settings and proper assignment:

Why does it appear only in TEST

Org structure (company codes) is maintained in CBC, but the detailed currency assignment lives in SSCUI 103379, which flows to TEST via your configuration transport/software collection. If 2830's currency assignment was completed in the configuration tenant, but the transport to TEST didn't carry it, CBC will show the company code as valid, while posting still fails. This matches "blocked only in TEST."

Constraints to verify:

  • In SSCUI 103379, drill into CC 2830 and confirm a complete currency-type assignment for every ledger it is assigned to, not just the leading ledger.
  • Confirm any freely defined / additional currency active on those ledgers has conversion settings maintained for 2830 (exchange rate type is configurable for all types except 10; type 10 is fixed to rate type M — KBA 3512760).
  • Check whether 2830 was recently created or copied from default content; copied company codes frequently keep the default functional currency (10) but miss the full assignment (KBA 2977199).
  • Confirm the relevant SSCUI 103379 transport actually reached the TEST tenant.

Summary: Open SSCUI 103379, go to the company-code-level currency assignment for CC 2830, and verify that the complete set of currency types (10 + 30 + any additional) is maintained for every ledger 2830 is assigned to. If the config looks correct in your configuration tenant but TEST still fails, re-transport that activity. The screenshots of SSCUI 103379 for CC 2830 (all ledgers) will pinpoint the missing type quickly.

Hope it helps.

Best regards,

Michal

entry.title: Next-gen identity with SAP IAG, SAP Access Control and Microsoft Entra
entry.content:
As SAP Identity Management (SAP IDM) approaches end of maintenance, many customers are asking an important question: what comes next?

For partners, this is more than a migration conversation. It is an opportunity to guide customers toward a modern identity architecture built for cloud, hybrid, and future business needs.

Join our upcoming webinar, “Beyond SAP Identity Management: Next-gen Identity with SAP IAG, SAP Access Control and Microsoft Entra,” and explore how SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance (SAP IAG) and Microsoft Entra work together to deliver compliant access governance and identity lifecycle management.

If you work with customers running SAP IDM today, or customers planning new SAP cloud implementations, this session will help you identify the right opportunities, position the target architecture, and qualify for the next stage of the enablement journey.

The conversations around identity are changing fast. This session will help you stay ahead and engage customers with confidence.

entry.title: Re: UDF Field Tab Order Navigation Issue in SAP Business One
entry.content: Thank you for testing and confirming. Option 1 — UI API Add-On (Permanent Fix) If you have or can develop a simple SAP B1 add-on, this is the only reliable solution. In the et_FORM_LOAD event, access the UDF form and set TabOrder explicitly:SAPbouiCOM.Form oUDFForm = oApp.Forms.Item(oForm.UDFFormUID); oUDFForm.Items.Item("U_Field1").TabOrder = 1; oUDFForm.Items.Item("U_Field2").TabOrder = 2; oUDFForm.Items.Item("U_Field3").TabOrder = 3; oUDFForm.Items.Item("U_Field4").TabOrder = 4;This works every time the form opens and does not require recreating UDFs.

entry.title: Re: Incorrect Exchange rates for Calculated costs
entry.content:
Dear @SiddheshParab,

PC02 Calculated Costs is determined from sales order costing / product cost planning, not from the standard sales order pricing exchange-rate logic used by other SD condition types. For sales order costing SAP confirms that the system normally uses costing variant PPC4 and valuation variant 001. For this valuation variant the relevant planning category is PLN and by default this planning category is mapped to exchange rate type P. Therefore, PC02 can show a different exchange-rate result than other sales order condition types if those conditions use a different exchange rate type or date

More details below:

SAP KBA 3513695 - CK323 error raised in sales order costing

SAP KBA 3667170 - How Is the Exchange Rate Selected During Material Cost Estimate in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

SAP KBA 3482860 - Unexpected exchange rate type used for material cost estimate - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

For comparison the normal sales/service order pricing exchange-rate type follows a different logic. SAP confirms that it can be taken from the sold-to party sales-area data in Manage Customer Master Data and if that field is blank the system defaults to exchange rate type M. This explains why PC02 may differ from other pricing conditions

More details below:

SAP KBA 3599406 - The determination of exchange rate type in sales and service orders - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Recommended checks:

  1. Verify the exchange rate maintained for exchange rate type P in the Currency Exchange Rates app
  2. Verify the mapping of planning category PLN in SSCUI 101970 Maintain Category for Planning
  3. Check the valuation/conversion date used by the sales order cost estimate, because PC02 follows costing logic rather than simply reusing the sales order pricing exchange rate

Summary:

PC02 is driven by sales order costing. In standard Public Cloud the key exchange-rate source is planning category PLN mapped to exchange rate type P, not the standard SD pricing exchange-rate logic

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: [S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition] Multi-approver workflow for supplier invoices by cost center
entry.content:
S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Multi-approver workflow for supplier invoices by cost center – how to configure?

We need to implement an approval workflow for MIRO-type supplier invoices (invoice verification / accounts payable) so that when an invoice is posted against a specific cost center, multiple people can approve or reject it — not just a single responsible person.

What we have explored so far:

  1. Cost center master data – Responsible Person field: This field only allows a single person to be assigned, so it doesn't cover the multi-approver scenario we need.
  2. "Manage Teams and Responsibilities" app: We found this application and explored it as a potential solution, but when configuring the Type and Category fields, we cannot find any standard option that maps to supplier invoice approval against a cost center. It's unclear whether this app is even intended for this process.

Specific questions:

  1. Is there a standard mechanism in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to define a group of approvers for supplier invoices based on the cost center being posted to?
  2. Can "Manage Teams and Responsibilities" be used for this purpose? If so, which Type/Category combination should be used?
  3. Is there any workflow configuration available in Public Cloud for this scenario, or is this a limitation of the public edition?

    Any guidance or pointers to relevant documentation would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Username, IAS, business and personal email addresses recommendations
entry.content:
Dear Community, 

we are facing a recurring situation with one of our customers. They would like some employees to be able to access to Successfactors through IAS but these employees don't have a business email address. 

Therefore we would want them to access with their personal email address. 

According to this KBA, mapping the business email address to the username is not recommended, although it can be done. 

We thought of creating a third email type (login email), and feed it via rules or integration: if no business email, feed it with the personal email address; otherwise feed it with the business email. Then map this email to the username, and send it to IAS to the LOGIN NAME. 

My question here is - what are SAP recommendations for those scenarios? I've been doing some unsuccessful research - this is not the first time we work with a company whose employees could not have a business email address. 

Thank you for your help

Regards

Laura

entry.title: CFDI Idoc Solution: Idoc not linked with billing document
entry.content:
Dear support-team,

usually outgouing Idocs are linked with the triggering document in SAP - for example:

Billing document triggers INVOICE02 Idoc. Outgoing Idoc is linked with billing document. You can see the link (or relationship)

  • in VF02/VF03 under Relationsships
  • WE02/WE05/BD87 under Relationships

For our mexicon company code we have customized the CFDI Idoc solution which also creates Idoc triggered by billing document. In this solution a different Idoc Typs is beeing used (MXCFDIREQ002). As we noticed this Idoc is not linked with the billing document as it is done for INVOIC02 Idocs.

Please provide information on how the Idocs for CFDI Idoc solution can be linked to the billing document.

Thanks in advance and best regards.
Alex Dürl

entry.title: Re: 財務諸表バージョンや利益センタグループの有効期間での表示内容確認
entry.content:
Hi Yosuke,

Short answer: "Not supported as a report hierarchy" does not mean you cannot select the FSV — it means the Trial Balance app consumes any hierarchy only as a generic G/L account grouping and ignores the FSV-specific reporting logic. Merging your two validity slices into one is neither required nor what drives this behaviour.

What "not supported as a report hierarchy" actually means: It refers to FSV semantics, not selectability.

The Trial Balance app (F0996A) and Trial Balance Comparison (F2767) accept a G/L account hierarchy — including one that is technically an FSV maintained in Manage Global Hierarchies — but they read it generically. Trial balances support general ledger account hierarchies in a generic way and don't need to be financial statement version hierarchies with contra node settings. Trial Balance

Concretely, the app does not apply the FSV-specific behaviour:

  • Contra node / balance-dependent display (the Debit/Credit Shift Between FSV Nodes logic). A contra node lets accounts appear under node A or node B depending on whether the balance is in credit or debit — the trial balance ignores this. Financial Statement Version
  • Special FSV nodes (Net Result, Not Assigned, P&L result calculation).

So "the app uses only the G/L account hierarchy" = it rolls balances up the node structure for grouping, nothing more. Being able to pick the FSV in the hierarchy selector is not a contradiction; it is simply read as a plain account grouping.

If you need true FSV behaviour: use the financial-statement apps (Balance Sheet/Income Statement), not the Trial Balance. The app supports time-dependent hierarchies and lets you select the FSV key date in the filter criteria to display the FSV of that date. Balance Sheet/Income Statement

Does merging the two active validity periods into one help?

No.

Two non-overlapping FSV time slices (yours: 2025-01-01→2025-12-31 and 2026-01-01→9999-12-31) are correct by design and should both stay Active. With the time-dependent nature of FSVs, you can have several hierarchy versions under one identical FSV ID, provided their time periods don't coincide. The system applies the version valid for the reporting key date — the 2025 slice for 2025 reports, the 2026 slice for 2026.

Collapsing them to one Active version would not enable FSV reporting logic in the trial balance (that logic is skipped regardless) and would destroy your historical 2025 structure. The two active slices are not the cause of the "not supported" statement.
Reference: FSV

Constraints to verify:

  • Key date vs system date: which slice is applied depends on the key date. In apps/transactions with no option to specify a key date, the valid hierarchy is derived from the current system date, and the selected fiscal period has no effect. Confirm your run picks the correct slice for past-year reports. Reference: Manage Global Hierarchies
  • Filtering requires two fields: to actually filter by the hierarchy in the Trial Balance app you must add both "G/L Account Hierarchy" and "G/L Account Hierarchy (Node)"; selecting only one returns unfiltered results. Reference: KBA 3355617 (related: KBA 3093135).
  • Status: a slice must be Active (not Draft) to be used at runtime.

Summary: Select the FSV in the trial balance only for generic account grouping — it will never reproduce balance-sheet semantics (contra nodes, net result). For statutory / FSV-structured output use Balance Sheet/Income Statement with the FSV key date. Keep both non-overlapping slices Active; do not merge them. If a past-year run shows the wrong structure, check the key-date/system-date behaviour and that both hierarchy filters are set.

Hope it helps.

Best Regards,

Michal

entry.title: Re: Conversion to S/4HANA: Issue with custom long texts and concatenated TDNAME including MATNR
entry.content:
Hi All!

Here is an update after raising an OSS-incident and receiving a helpful answer from support:

  • The program which does the update for impacted standard long texts during the system conversion to S/4HANA is MFLE_XPRA_STXH
  • It selects the relevant long texts based on a combination of constants provided in the code and entries in table MFLE_XPRA_MAPPER taking status information in MFLE_XPRA_STATUS into account to ensure that the update is only tackled once for the entries
  • The update of the key field TDNAME happens via a kernel function based on a dynamic SQL-statement build during the process

Based on this information I did a PoC in a sandbox system via debugging during which I added corresponding entries for our custom text ID in the internal table containing the selected data from MFLE_XPRA_MAPPER. These additional table entries were the only tweaks needed to get TDNAME also updated successfully for our custom long texts. Due to the entries in the status table no other long texts were impacted.

So, in order to do these updates for real, a few relevant entries would need to be added to table MFLE_XPRA_MAPPER via a direct table update (as the table is not open to configuration via SM30) sometime after the system conversion. This would however basically be at the customer's own risk as this conversion logic is limited to standard SAP text types. For custom (non-SAP) text types, no standard conversion or solution is provided by SAP. Therefore, any necessary adjustments (table MFLE_XPRA_MAPPER) or data handling for such custom text types must be managed independently on the customer side. 

As of right now, we haven't yet tried this via a direct table update and rerunning the conversion program, but if we decide to try it, we luckily enough have a few sandbox systems available in which we can try it to ensure that the updates then also work as expected once the productive systems are getting converted.

"No risk - no fun" - but also proceed with caution at your own risk if you are in a similar situation with custom long texts relying on a concatenation which includes the material number!

Hope this is helpful for others!

Cheers

Bärbel

@LukaszPegiel @VXLozano @Ryan-Crosby @RaymondGiuseppi

entry.title: Re: wage type is not picking up while running remittance
entry.content:
I'm not familiar with Payroll for Country Code 10, but here are 2 suggestions:

Through the IMG (t-code = spro) you could make a test of copying your new WageType in order to list the tables that would be impacted, and then do an other test copying an existing Remittance WageType to compare the tables that would be impacted.  For all those tables, comfirm that the configuration is the same. 

Then, use program RPDASC00 to expand your Payroll Schema and it's Rules, and search for PCRs that would process the existing Remittance WageType but not your new existing WageType.

entry.title: CN72 is not capturing the ECP Data for PS Projects.
entry.content:
The current snapshot functionality available through transaction CN72 does not capture Easy Cost Planning (ECP) entries from the CKIS table.

To address this limitation, we are considering developing a custom program that extracts CKIS table entries and stores them in a custom Z-table, along with relevant date and timestamp information. 
This solution would enable us to maintain historical snapshots and support comparison with live data in Fiori reports.

We would appreciate your guidance on whether there are any standard SAP options available to capture snapshots of Easy Cost Planning (ECP) data.

If no standard solution exists, please confirm whether proceeding with the custom approach—capturing CKIS records along with date and timestamp in a Z-table—would be appropriate.
Additionally, we would like to highlight a concern regarding the potential growth of records in the snapshot table over time.

We would appreciate recommendations on how to efficiently manage data volume and ensure optimal performance as the dataset increases, Approx. Data Volume 1Million Records per Month.
with the Retention Period of 18Months to pull the data frequently and show the same in the Comparision Reports.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business AI Platform partner-led adoption incentive
entry.content:
This is an important initiative, and SAP Business AI adoption will absolutely need strong partner-led execution.

That said, I want to share a real emerging-partner perspective. Many capable specialist partners are actively investing in SAP Business AI, BTP, Joule-aligned use cases, Cloud ALM, automation, clean-core execution, and PoC-to-production offerings.

However, in practice, the path can be difficult to navigate. Access to incentive programs often appears tied to PartnerEdge status, while entry into the relevant PartnerEdge track itself can take time, involve multiple steps, and require clearer guidance. That creates a difficult loop for emerging partners: follow the official partner path, but access to AI incentives, enablement sessions, sandbox/TDD environments, and technical guidance may depend on already being fully inside the ecosystem.

This is especially challenging because SAP partnership is not free. Emerging partners invest real money, certifications, resources, business planning, and thousands of dollars to build SAP-aligned capabilities. So when the ecosystem is described as open, there also needs to be a clearer and more practical onboarding path for qualified specialist partners who are already investing.

The future of SAP Business AI cannot be driven only by the largest established partners. Customers also need lean, AI-first specialist firms that can move fast, build focused PoCs, support clean-core execution, and help convert AI ambition into measurable business outcomes.

I hope SAP uses this incentive program not only to fund adoption, but also to improve access, routing, enablement, and transparency for capable emerging partners who want to help SAP customers succeed.

entry.title: Working with Large Data Models in SAP Datasphere Using Claude Code
entry.content:
Where We Left Off

A few weeks ago, my colleague @Yonggang_Yu published a great blog post: Using Claude Code and Natural Language to Create SAP Datasphere Artifacts

I loved the idea: describe what you need in plain language, and Claude Code translates that into the right CLI commands to create tables, views, and analytic models in Datasphere. I forked his repo and started playing around with it.

It worked really well. But after a while, I ran into a limitation.

The Problem: You Rarely Start from Scratch

When I started testing, I was creating fresh tables and views. That's fun, but it's not what most real projects look like.

In practice, you're usually working inside an existing data model. Maybe it's been growing for months. There are 50 tables, 120 views, 3 analytic models and they all depend on each other in ways that aren't always obvious. A business requirement comes in: "we need to add a country code to the reporting layer." Or a data architect says a column needs to be renamed to match a new naming standard.

That's when the original skills hit a wall. They're great at creating new objects, but they don't help you understand what you already have, or how a change to one object ripples through everything downstream.

I wanted to solve that problem. I used the SAP Group Reporting data model in Datasphere as my test case as it's a real-world, complex model with a deep chain of views layered on top of consolidated finance tables. Exactly the kind of thing you'd find in a production environment. And I happened to have worked on it recently and knew it fairly well. Also, this data model is available to anyone who wants to try for themselves.

The Challenge: Dependency Chains and Circular Deadlocks

To understand why this is hard, you need to know a bit about how Datasphere enforces consistency.

When you have a chain like this:

SAP_FI_SOURCE_TABLE
    └── SAP_FI_INTERMEDIATE_VIEW
            └── SAP_FI_REPORTING_VIEW
                    └── AM_FINANCE

...and you want to add a new column, you can't just add it to the source table and call it done. Every view in the chain needs to be updated to pass that column through. With 4 objects that's annoying. With 20 objects it's a real problem and takes a lot of time in the Datasphere UI.

Renaming or removing a column is even harder. Datasphere enforces referential integrity on every save. If you try to rename a column on a view, DSP blocks it with an HTTP 422 error, because downstream objects still reference the old name. But if you try to update the downstream object first, DSP blocks that too - because the upstream view doesn't have the new name yet.

The solution is a flag called --allow-missing-dependencies. It tells Datasphere: "save this, even if some references don't resolve yet, they'll be fixed shortly." With this flag, the deadlock breaks. Order stops mattering. You can update all objects freely and Datasphere sorts it out once everything is saved.

Figuring this out and baking it into every skill was one of the key things I had to get right.

The New Skills

I added five new skills on top of Yonggang's original six. Together they cover the "working inside an existing model" use case.

impact-analysis - understand before you change

Before touching anything, you (and Claude!) want to know what you're dealing with. This skill scans the entire space, builds a dependency graph of every view and analytic model, and shows you the full upstream/downstream chain for any object.

You tell Claude Code:

"What objects depend on SAP_FI_IL_A_ConsolidationUnit? Show me everything downstream."

Claude runs the impact-analysis skill and you get a tree like this in the terminal:

SAP_FI_IL_A_ConsolidationUnit [view]
  └── SAP_FI_ConsolidationUnit [view]          (direct)
        └── SAP_FI_IL_T_ConsolidationUnit [view]  (direct)
              └── AM_ConsolidationUnit [AM]         (direct)

Every object is tagged by type ([table], [view], [AM]), ordered from closest to furthest. You can also ask "which of these views are missing column COUNTRY_CODE?" and the skill highlights exactly which ones need updating, giving you a ready-made action plan before a single change is made.

The first run reads the whole space (~2-3 minutes for 100+ objects). After that, results are cached and every subsequent query is instant.

propagate-columns - add a column end-to-end

Once you've added a column to a source table, this skill cascades it through every downstream view automatically. You tell Claude:

"Add COUNTRY_CODE (3-char string) to SAP_FI_IL_A_ConsolidationUnit and make sure it appears in all downstream views."

Claude seeds the table, then runs the propagation. The terminal shows progress as each view is updated and verified:

[1/4] SAP_FI_ConsolidationUnit          → updated ✓
[2/4] SAP_FI_IL_T_ConsolidationUnit     → updated ✓
[3/4] SAP_FI_IL_A_ConsolidationUnitHierDir → updated ✓
[4/4] AM_ConsolidationUnit              → updated ✓

All objects verified. Deploying in topological order...
Deploy complete.

It walks the graph in topological order (sources before consumers), updates each view, verifies the column is present, then deploys everything in sequence. You can also ask Claude to do a dry run first and it will print the full plan without touching anything.

rename-column-cascade - rename across the whole chain

You tell Claude:

"Rename CNSLDTN_UNIT to CONSOLIDATION_UNIT in SAP_FI_REPORTING_VIEW and all objects that depend on it."

Claude renames the column in the starting view, then propagates the change through every downstream view and analytic model. The --allow-missing-dependencies flag is used on every save automatically, so the circular deadlock described above never becomes your problem.

remove-column-cascade - remove across the whole chain

Same pattern as rename, but for removing a column. Useful when cleaning up deprecated fields that have spread across many views.

remove-column-cascade - remove across the whole chain

Same pattern as rename, but for removing a column. Useful when cleaning up deprecated fields that have spread across many views. Just tell Claude which column to remove and where to start and it handles the rest.

add-columns-to-table and add-columns-to-view

The building blocks used by the cascade skills above. These update a single object precisely - all three locations that Datasphere requires for graphical views (element definitions, SELECT projection, and the visual UI model). Getting any of these out of sync causes Datasphere to silently convert your graphical view to SQL mode, which is hard to recover from.

A Concrete Example: Group Reporting

Let me walk through a realistic scenario using the Group Reporting Data Model which is available as SAP Content.

The model has a deep chain: source integration tables feed intermediate views, which feed reporting views, which feed analytic models. A typical request: "The CDS Views in the connected S4 System have new columns, these need to be replicated to DSP and propagated all the way up to the analytic model."

Step 1: understand what's downstream

"Show me everything downstream of SAP_FIN_CS_IL_I_CNSLDTNGROUPSTRUCTURE_2, and check which objects are missing the column SEGMENT_CODE."

Claude runs impact-analysis and prints the dependency tree with a gap report at the bottom:

SAP_FIN_CS_IL_I_CNSLDTNGROUPSTRUCTURE_2 [table]
  └── SAP_FIN_CS_IL_A_ConsolidationUnit [view]      SEGMENT_CODE: missing ✗
        └── SAP_FIN_CS_ConsGroup [view]              SEGMENT_CODE: missing ✗
              └── SAP_FIN_CS_IL_T_ConsGroup [view]   SEGMENT_CODE: missing ✗
                    └── AM_GroupReporting [AM]        SEGMENT_CODE: missing ✗

Action plan: 4 objects need updating.

Step 2: make the change

"Add SEGMENT_CODE (string, 10 chars, label 'Segment Code') to that table and propagate it to all downstream views."

Claude seeds the source table, then cascades the column through the chain, updating, verifying, and deploying each object in order:

Adding SEGMENT_CODE to SAP_FIN_CS_IL_I_CNSLDTNGROUPSTRUCTURE_2... ✓

Propagating downstream:
[1/4] SAP_FIN_CS_IL_A_ConsolidationUnit    → updated ✓
[2/4] SAP_FIN_CS_ConsGroup                 → updated ✓
[3/4] SAP_FIN_CS_IL_T_ConsGroup            → updated ✓
[4/4] AM_GroupReporting                    → updated ✓

All objects verified. Deploying in topological order...
Deploy complete.

What would have been an hour of manual UI work (opening each view, adding the column in three places, saving, deploying) runs unattended in a couple of minutes.

Limitations

This is still early stage work, and it's worth being honest about where it falls short.

The skills cover the most common operations well, but Claude doesn't always take the direct path. Sometimes it runs into loops, asks for permissions repeatedly, or tries to figure things out step by step when the skill should have handled it cleanly. This is partly a prompting problem, partly a maturity problem - the more you use it, the better you get at describing what you want clearly.

More importantly: always check what Claude did. The cascade skills make real changes to real objects and deploy them. The backups are there for a reason. Before calling something done, open a few of the affected views in the Datasphere UI and verify the column is actually there, the view still renders in graphical mode, and nothing looks off. Automated doesn't mean infallible.

SQL views are a known gap - they can't be edited programmatically, so if one sits in the middle of a dependency chain, the cascade stops there and you have to finish manually. This could probably be solved by additional skills, but I have not looked into it yet.

What's Next

There's a lot of room to grow here. Better SQL view support, smarter conflict resolution, maybe skills that read a requirements doc and figure out what needs changing on their own, like Yonggang mentioned at the end of his Blog Post. I'm planning to keep adding things as I run into new use cases, and I'd genuinely welcome contributions from anyone who hits a gap and wants to fill it.

The code for all new skills is on GitHub: https://github.com/FredericWall/dsp-cli
Feel free to check the repo out, any contribution would be appreciated 🙂

Thanks to Yonggang for the original project and the idea. This is built directly on top of his work.

entry.title: Re: Journal entries with 0 interest amount are not displa...
entry.content:
Dear @yukitsumura,

This is consistent with standard behavior. Manage Interest Runs displays and manages calculated interest results, not every source journal entry evaluated by the job. Therefore, if the calculated interest amount is 0.00, there is no interest result amount to display in the app

More details below:

SAP Help – Schedule Interest Calculation Jobs
SAP Help – Manage Interest Runs
SAP Help – Item Interest Calculation Process

There is no documented standard configuration or parameter in Public Cloud to force true 0.00 interest items to be displayed in Manage Interest Runs. If this visibility is required in the standard app it should be raised as an improvement request through SAP Customer Influence

More details below:

SAP KBA 2963059 - How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: SAP Business Network customers explain why the solution continues to be Top Rated
entry.content:
For several years, SAP has partnered with TrustRadius, an HG Insights Company, to gather feedback from our customers about their satisfaction with our products. Thanks to these customers and the dozens more who contributed, SAP Business Network has, once again, been awarded Top Rated status in May 2026 for both Procurement and Supply Chain Visibility.  

According to Rajat Bhatnagar, SVP, Growth at HG Insights, “Consistent customer praise is harder to earn than any analyst ranking, and that’s exactly what the Top Rated award measures. SAP Business Network earning Top Rated in Procurement and Supply Chain Visibility reflects how much buyers and suppliers rely on it when operations actually matter. Reviewers point to real-time data visibility, invoice processing that reduces errors, and analytics that help teams spot supply chain issues before they turn into problems worth explaining to leadership.” 

We appreciate this recognition from TrustRadius, along with our many customers who left ratings and reviews like the following on the site: 

“SAP Business Network is one of the most impactful and visionary services, and it is an undefeated beast in the category. I am using it to monitor purchase order status and supplier commitments in real time to coordinate with procurement and finance teams on vendor selection and invoice approval, and along with that, it helps us in managing supply chain, cost, and also compliance-related matters. It is one of a kind and has always been so effective in each and every use case for us. We feel so lucky to have this service as our chosen one—a must-have for businesses.” 

Thank you to our buyer customers who find and discover trading partners on the platform, diligently support them in their onboarding process, and leverage the comprehensive capabilities of SAP Business Network – across procurement, supply chain, logistics, asset management, and finance – on a daily basis. In addition, our millions of supplier customers, in turn, stay on top of product orders, inventory, quality, and service requests on SAP Business Network to ensure that customers receive goods and services on time and according to negotiated agreements. Through tight collaboration, these buyers and suppliers deploy supply chains that are now more resilient, compliant, and efficient. Not only are these businesses connecting, growing, and thriving, but also they are providing critical products to end-customers. 

These customers express the impact of SAP Business Network on their businesses through their voluntary reviews: 

“[SAP Business Network] definitely helped us in building a stronger supply chain with our suppliers and maintain a good coordination. It also improved our invoicing accuracy and payment processing time simultaneously at the same time.” 

“I monitor real-time inventory and process purchase orders from major supermarket chains with accurate inventory levels. This allows me to anticipate stockouts and track them with logistics and the customer.” 

We are very excited to see these responses from our customers, and we greatly appreciate them taking the time to share their experience with others. Some of the business outcomes our customers have discussed in their reviews include the following:" 

  • “We have been able to reduce our procurement processing time by more than 38% this year.” 
  • “Overall operational productivity has been increased by more than 35% this year.” 
  • “We had 10x growth in our annual revenue by using SAP Business Network.” 
  • “Reduced the cost for emergency shipment by 40%” 

We can’t sum it up any better than this customer, who commented on TrustRadius:

“SAP Business Network has streamlined collaboration with our trading partners through order tracking and compliance checks. Real-time updates on orders and analytics for supplier performance are reducing delays and enhancing delivery reliability. Shared documentation simplifies fostering transparency and trust. This efficient platform has increased our supplier relationships, making operations smoother and aligning our goals with our trading partners.” 

It is that collaboration and trading partner alignment that we strive to enable with SAP Business Network. Visit us online to learn more.  To hear more from our customers, visit our reviews on Trust Radius.

entry.title: Re: SAP PR1 - Finished Goods Inventory Clearing Multiple...
entry.content:
In your case, the quantities showing in MD04 for those old purchase orders (POs) indicate that the system still considers the stock “in transit” — meaning the goods receipt has not been fully posted or cleared. This is common when the GR (Goods Receipt) was never completed or reversed properly for inbound deliveries or stock transport orders.

Possible Solutions

entry.title: Re: Replication Flow Blog Series Part 4 - Sizing
entry.content:
Hello,

I have some trouble with premium outbound....

The sum of the transfer for the replication task run detail that you can see now (2025.13) in detail section
is not equal to the summarized consumption at replication flow level.....

The total to me seems too high compared to the sum of the detailed transfers (by a factor of 2x - 2.5x)....

So...

Considering that this kind of billing is very very very costly and not proportional to the infrastructure cost...

Can we have the detailed measures on how it is calculated??


Thanks,
Alberto
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

I find the solution here

https://me.sap.com/notes/3470559

The size is the "uncompressed size". So the figures shown in the detail of replication flow are the size of parquet written,
while the true consumption is on the uncompressed size.

A rough estimation for a Full INIT of ACDOCA table with 1 billion rows: 

10^9 Rows x 1.5KB (Byte per record, an average record for me is 900B, but the NULL value count 1 Byte as per SAP note)

=> 1.5* 10^12 B = 1500 GB 
=> 1500 / 20 = 75 POI Blocks

I didn't know a full copy of 1Bn row ACDOCA will costs as much as a car...  

And this is just 1 init of 1 table... we can add MATDOC, CKIS, COEP_ORI, and so on...

1 table with all columns projected =~ 1 car
